entries.red_evidence: The canary models exported snapshot aliasing live mutable state; the W1 guard must fail if live membership mutation changes the captured export.
The canary represents a skipped membership tail after snapshot restore; W2 convergence must go red when the tail is not replayed.
The failpoint canary downgrades a snapshot apply error; W3 must fail rather than accept a stale runtime.
The failpoint helper replaces a captured snapshot with a live export; the W4 falsifiability test asserts the paired guards panic.
The negative manifest fixture tries to close unexplained apply errors as environmental; the ledger guard must reject it.
The bounded progress guard must reject a frozen peer that can block the raft drive loop.
The fast-suite gate must reject an invented baseline and an unreviewed budget overrun.
The CI governance guard must reject a missing job or step.
The canary models accepting a stale member set after restore; W7 membership convergence must reject that state.
The canary accepts a stale-term snapshot; the corpus guard must fail if stale snapshots are treated as valid.
The canary models corrupt bytes accepted as valid; W9 checksum guards must fail this state.
The canary serves stale local membership after InstallSnapshot; W10 must catch the stale view.
The canary makes a failed snapshot save leave visible partial state; W11 must reject partial visibility.
The canary tail-skip state must be caught by at least one exhaustive grid cell.
The canary double-applies a retried ConfChange; W13 idempotency must fail this outcome.
The canary allows two leaders in one term; W14 clock-skew safety must fail that outcome.
The canary injects an untriaged survivor line; W15 must fail the mutation baseline check.
The canary models mutable Arc aliasing across export; W16 Miri-safe deep-clone guard must fail that shape.
The canary registry fixture lists an inert canary; W17 must reject entries where makes_guard_fail is false.
The canary models a shrinker that returns a non-reproducing schedule; W18 must require exact replay and one-step-minimal reproducing schedules.
The canary models loading bad_seeds.json without replaying it; W19 must count every corpus entry as executed.
The canary removes categories from the corpus table; W20 must fail when any required etcd edge category is missing.
The canary seeds a lost committed command; W21 must report the no-lost-committed-entry invariant violation.
The canary replays the same committed trace with deterministic random eviction and proves it violates the Belady tolerance; W22 must fail if the hit-rate bound stops discriminating.
The canary runs the same bounded model with a faulty snapshot-install transition that drops the last committed entry; W23 must discover a no-committed-entry-lost counterexample.
The canary models a broken corpus runner that counts zero executed seeds; W24 must require committed corpus size and replay count to match.
The canary fixture models a broken oracle that accepts a known lost-write history; W25 must reject the same history with the real sequential-witness checker.
The canary swaps mutual exclusion for relaxed load/store acquisition; W26 loom guards must find an interleaving with multiple winners.
The canary deliberately leaks the only in-flight ticket; W27 resource-chaos guards must observe that the pool remains exhausted instead of returning to baseline.
The canary builds two committed-mode views with different members and command counts; W28 differential guards must reject a checker that passes stale-mode disagreement.
The invariant catalog must reject a handoff that regresses a committed view.
The snapshot-delivery guard must reject applying a delayed stale snapshot.
The durable recovery guard must reject valid bytes belonging to the wrong node.
The compatibility guard must reject silently regenerated previous-release goldens.
The gated-test registry must reject incomplete proof registrations.
The cache matrix must reject stale loader completion after invalidation.
The adapter corpus must reject invalidation emitted for a rolled-back write.
The configuration guard must reject credential bytes in diagnostics.
The resource budget must reject leaked child and connection handles.
The independent TLC model must produce its reviewed invariant counterexample under UnsafeSecondLeader.
The cancellation-safety guard must fail when the deliberately non-cancel-safe fixture leaks a permit at a registered checkpoint.
